page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance type built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz. The instance provides 16 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, and it does not include any GPU hardware. Network connectivity is rated at a baseline bandwidth of 10 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations place the ecs.g7.4xlarge consistently in the average performance tier. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance is measured at 1889.28, while the multi-core score reaches 15481.55. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 835.37 GB/sec, which drops to 94.57 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, and a write speed of 55.52 GB/sec. In application-specific tests, the instance achieves a Geekbench multi-core score of 9505.0 and a Passmark database operations score of 7892.12. Redis SET operations perform at 15.62 million operations per second. For machine learning workloads, the instance processes 1294.06 tokens per second for 135M model prompts, but performance drops to a weak 31.85 tokens per second for medium 7B model prompt processing.

From a resource density perspective, the 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio offers a balanced hardware profile for general-purpose workloads without the premium cost of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated instances. However, the lack of local storage requires users to attach external block storage, which may introduce latency and additional cost considerations. The instance is configured for workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, text processing, and moderate database operations, but it is not recommended for large-scale LLM inference or storage-heavy applications.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor at 2.7 GHz. It includes 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Network performance is anchored by a 10 Gbps baseline bandwidth. Benchmark results place this instance in the average tier, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 9505.0 and a Redis SET throughput of 15.62 million operations per second. While it handles small-model LLM inference and software compilation efficiently, prompt processing for 7B models is relatively weak at 31.85 tokens per second. The lack of local storage represents a key architectural tradeoff, requiring external storage attachments. This instance is suited for general-purpose workloads, including static web serving, software compilation, and database operations.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 10 Gbps bandwidth, delivering balanced mid-tier performance for databases and web serving.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7.4xlarge features 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 10 Gbps bandwidth for balanced general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 compute platform featuring dedicated Intel Xeon cores and a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio for general-purpose workloads.

64.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
